scenario.description: Required top-level mode indicator for the IR. 'migration' = brownfield / reverse-engineered from a source platform; preserved artifacts under source.artifactsRoot are copied byte-for-byte (Article II.a). 'greenfield' = authored from PRD; no source artifacts to preserve.

sourcePlatform.description: Echo of source.platform at metadata level for quick agent lookup. When metadata.scenario is 'migration', this MUST equal source.platform.

source.description: Provenance of this integration. Reverse-engineered integrations (BizTalk, MuleSoft, TIBCO, Boomi, ...) MUST populate this so platform packs can apply source-aware fast paths — e.g. for `platform: biztalk` the Azure pack consumes flat-file XSDs verbatim (preserving `b:` annotations) via `FlatFileDecoding`/`FlatFileEncoding`, runs BizTalk-compiled XSLT (including `<msxsl:script>` / `userCSharp`) as-is via the `Xslt` action, and treats a missing `nativeSchemaRef` / `mappings[].codeRef` as Sev-1 rather than silently degrading. Greenfield integrations MUST NOT include this block.

artifactsRoot.description: Folder (relative to the integration folder) that holds extracted native artifacts (XSDs, XSLTs, ODX, BTP, helper .cs). `messages[].nativeSchemaRef` and `mappings[].codeRef` resolve against this root when not absolute. For BizTalk this is typically `specs/biztalk/_extracted/` (msi-cracker output) or `artifacts/custom/`.

preservedRoot.description: Optional absolute or relative path to the root of the original source-platform solution (e.g. C:/Projects/BizTalk-Combined). When set, `messages[].nativeSchemaSource.path` and `mappings[].sourceArtifact.*` paths are resolved against this root in preference to artifactsRoot.

inventoryRef.description: Optional path to the source-platform inventory file (e.g. `specs/biztalk/biztalk-inventory.md`).

manifestRef.description: Optional path to a machine-readable manifest of extracted artifacts (e.g. `specs/biztalk/_extracted/_manifest.json` from biztalk-msi-cracker).

audience.description: The OAuth2 audience / resource identifier the access token must be issued for.

scopes.description: List of OAuth2 scopes required by this channel.

clientCredentials.description: Optional client-credentials hint. clientId is a param reference; clientSecret must be a secret-store reference, never a literal.

clientIdRef.description: Parameter or environment variable name holding the client ID.

clientSecretRef.description: Secret-store reference (e.g. Key Vault URI). Never a literal value.

tags.description: Free-form labels for cross-referencing (e.g. catalogue IDs like INT-001, domain tags). Agents and visualizers use these to correlate artifacts across pipeline stages.

auth.description: Authentication scheme for this channel. Either a well-known enum value or an OAuth2 configuration object.

subscription.description: Topic subscription. Legacy string form keeps the subscription name; object form adds a broker-native filter (SQL, correlation, or header). Packs compile the filter to the platform's subscription rule (Service Bus SQL filter, SNS filter policy, etc.).

binding.description: Broker-native metadata. Shape depends on channel.kind. Unsupported fields must be surfaced by packs as Sev-2 'feature not supported by pack' findings rather than silently dropped. See docs/ir-spec.md 'Channel bindings cookbook' for kind-specific fields.

consumer.description: Selective- and competing-consumer controls on inbound queue/topic channels. count is the number of concurrent consumer instances (defaults to the pack baseline); selector is a JSONata / SQL filter evaluated on the message envelope.

delivery.description: Delivery guarantee for this channel. Packs must compile it to the closest native primitive and ir-validator rejects exactly-once on brokers that can't support it (http, eventgrid).

ordering.description: Message ordering guarantee. 'byKey' partitions by orderingKey/sessionKey; 'fifo' is global order; 'none' imposes no order.

orderingKey.description: Body path (e.g. 'body.orderId') or header name (e.g. 'X-Session-Id') used to partition messages when ordering is 'byKey'. Required by ir-validator whenever ordering is 'byKey'.

sessionKey.description: Broker session key (Service Bus, JMS). Acts as an alias for ordering=byKey on session-aware brokers; packs may set it alongside orderingKey.

ack.description: Acknowledgement mode for a consumer on a queue/topic channel. 'peekLock' is the safe default on Service Bus / Kafka pull; 'manual' requires the flow to settle explicitly; 'auto' settles on receive.

concurrency.description: Consumer concurrency controls for inbound queue/topic channels.

maxDeliveryCount.description: Broker-level redelivery limit before the message is dead-lettered. Distinct from errorHandling.retry.count (which is flow-level).

ttl.description: Channel-level default message time-to-live as an ISO-8601 duration (e.g. PT1H, P7D).

retention.description: How long the broker retains messages on this channel as an ISO-8601 duration (e.g. P30D). Article V compliance hook — used by reviewer to check PRD retention clauses.

classification.description: Data classification of messages crossing this channel. Overrides message-level classification when present; the stricter of the two wins during review.

tags.description: Free-form labels for cross-referencing (e.g. catalogue IDs like INT-001).

nativeSchemaRef.description: DEPRECATED — alias of nativeSchemaSource.path with implicit origin: preserved. Kept for back-compat with greenfield-era IRs. New IRs MUST use nativeSchemaSource. Path to the original native schema (XSD, flat-file XSD, EDI XSD, Avro .avsc, Protobuf .proto) relative to the integration folder.

nativeSchemaSource.description: Replacement for nativeSchemaRef. Declares whether the runtime schema artifact is preserved verbatim from the source platform (origin: preserved) or generated by the pipeline (origin: generated). path is resolved against source.preservedRoot when present, otherwise source.artifactsRoot, otherwise the IR file folder.

format.description: Wire format of the message body. Packs use this to pick the right parser/serializer and the default mapping engine.

encoding.description: Character or binary encoding of the body. Defaults to utf-8 for text formats.

schemaLanguage.description: Language of the document referenced by schemaRef. Informs the validator a pack must run.

headers.description: Message metadata that lives outside the body (e.g. Service Bus / AMQP properties, HTTP headers, Kafka headers).

examples.description: Named example payloads. Each path is resolved relative to the IR file.

idempotencyKey.description: Body path (e.g. 'body.orderId') or header name (e.g. 'X-Idempotency-Key') that uniquely identifies this message for de-duplication. Required by Article III on any message consumed from an external source.

dedupWindow.description: How long a consumer must remember the idempotencyKey, as an ISO-8601 duration (e.g. PT10M, P1D).

correlationId.description: Body path or header name carrying the end-to-end correlation id. Structural (not header-only) so flows can reference it uniformly across transports.

version.description: Semver of this message contract. Bump when mapping a breaking change.

compatibility.description: Schema evolution policy for this message (Confluent-style): backward = new readers can read old data; forward = old readers can read new data; full = both; none = breaking changes allowed.

classification.description: Data classification for the whole message body. Mapping rules may override per field.

pii.description: Whether this message, as a whole, carries personally-identifiable information. Per-field overrides live on mapping rules.

source.description: Where the pack should populate this header from.

value.description: Literal value, JSONata expression, or body path. Interpretation depends on source.

name.description: PascalCase mapping identifier, referenced from flow steps via mappingRef.

tags.description: Free-form labels for cross-referencing (e.g. catalogue IDs like INT-001).

engine.description: Transformation language the mapping is expressed in. Default JSONata; packs must compile to a platform-native equivalent. Use 'custom' when the mapping is expressed in source-platform code (scripting functoid, DataWeave, Groovy, etc.) that cannot be represented in a standard engine.

expression.description: Whole-document transformation in the chosen engine. Mutually exclusive with rules and codeRef.

rules.description: Field-level rules. Mutually exclusive with expression and codeRef. Used when a per-field mapping table is clearer than a single expression.

codeRef.description: Path to the extracted source-code artifact (relative to the IR file). Required when engine is 'custom'. The file is stored in the repo for audit and as input to reverse-engineering tooling.

runtime.description: Execution environment required by the custom code. Used by packs to decide how to wrap or invoke it.

migrationHint.description: Pack-facing migration verdict set by the reverse-engineering agent. 'preserve' = do not regenerate, copy verbatim from sourceArtifact (requires origin: preserved); 'auto' = pack can compile natively; 'local-function' = emit an in-process function wrapper; 'azure-function' = sidecar required (Sev-2 finding); 'manual' = STRUCTURAL blocker, pack must not emit output for this flow.

origin.description: Whether this mapping is preserved from the source platform (origin: preserved — compiler MUST copy sourceArtifact verbatim and apply only the declared transforms[<target>] adapter; in-IR `rules` are documentation only) or authored by the pipeline (origin: authored — compiler MUST compile rules/expression to a platform-native transform).

sourceArtifact.description: Typed alternative to codeRef. Each property names a source-platform artifact format and points to the file (resolved against source.preservedRoot then source.artifactsRoot). Required when origin is 'preserved' (at least one of sourceArtifact or codeRef).

xslt.description: XSLT 1.0/2.0/3.0 stylesheet. May be a file:// URI when the artifact lives outside the workspace.

csharp.description: C# source (helper class, scripting functoid extract).

transforms.description: Per-target compilation strategy, keyed by target platform pack id (e.g. logic_apps_standard, mulesoft, azure_functions). Value names the adapter the pack applies to sourceArtifact: 'passthrough' = copy unchanged; 'xslt_to_dataweave' = re-express as DataWeave; 'xslt_to_local_function' = wrap in an in-proc .NET local function; 'xslt_to_azure_function' = host as a sidecar Azure Function; 'regenerate' = compiler regenerates from in-IR rules.

parameters.description: External transform parameters with workflow bindings. Each entry declares an input the preserved transform expects (e.g. an XSLT global parameter) and the workflow expression that supplies the value at runtime.

name.description: Parameter name as referenced inside the transform (e.g. an XSLT <xsl:param name="..."/>).

lookups.description: Reference tables usable from rules via `lookup: <name>` or from expressions via $lookup('name', key).

tests.description: Golden-file tests executed by packs (and CI) to prove a mapping produces the expected target shape.

messageRef.description: Must match an entry in messages[].name.

contentPath.description: Optional path into the referenced message (e.g. `body`, `body.order`, `/Envelope/Body/Order`).

target.description: Dotted path (JSON) or XPath (XML) to the target field this rule writes.

source.description: Dotted path (JSON) or XPath (XML) on the source document.

expression.description: Engine expression used to compute the target value. Takes precedence over source+lookup.

default.description: Literal fallback when source is missing / expression yields null. May be any JSON value.

condition.description: Engine predicate; rule is only applied when it evaluates truthy.

lookup.description: Name of a lookup in mappings[].lookups; used when source is a key into a reference table.

classification.description: Per-field classification override. Stricter than the containing message's classification.

pii.description: Whether this field carries personally-identifiable information.

redact.description: Redaction applied when compiling to a platform-native masking primitive (Logic Apps secure inputs, Boomi mask, MuleSoft obfuscate). Required by pii-flow-checker when a pii field crosses a classification: public boundary.

sourceRef.description: Path to an external reference-data file (JSON/YAML) containing the lookup table. Mutually exclusive with entries.

method.description: HTTP method for this endpoint. Authoritative over OpenAPI; contract-designer projects it into the OpenAPI document and ir-validator fails on divergence.

path.description: HTTP path (e.g. '/orders'). When the bound channel carries a binding.path, the two must agree.

requestBody.description: Request-body descriptor. messageRef must match an entry in messages[].

responses.description: Keyed by HTTP status code. Each entry may carry a messageRef and content types; contract-designer projects these into OpenAPI responses.

parameters.description: Path, query, and header parameters for this endpoint.

idempotencyHeader.description: Header name the endpoint expects to carry the idempotency key (e.g. 'Idempotency-Key'). Packs propagate the inbound message's idempotencyKey into this header.

rateLimit.description: Endpoint-level rate limit. Overrides channel.binding.rateLimit when both are present.

cors.description: CORS policy. Packs compile to their native CORS primitive (APIM, Logic Apps, etc.).

contractTests.description: Optional pact-style producer/consumer fixtures. contract-linter runs them when the pact CLI is on PATH; degrades to a Sev-2 'runtime missing' finding otherwise.

role.description: Whether this integration is the pact consumer or provider for the fixture.

pactFile.description: Path to a pact JSON file, resolved relative to the IR.

tags.description: Free-form labels for cross-referencing (e.g. catalogue IDs like INT-001).

implementation.description: Target compute host for this flow on the active platform pack. Set by the target-architecture agent based on ais-platform / data-factory / azure-functions / batch-processing skills, then consumed by the platform pack's /implement-* prompt to dispatch the right compiler. When absent, packs default to their primary host (Azure pack -> logic-app-standard).

host.description: Compiler dispatch key. logic-app-standard = azure-logic-apps-compiler; function-app = azure-functions-compiler (stand-alone .NET 8 isolated worker); data-factory = azure-data-factory-compiler. Other values reserved for future packs.

hostingPlan.description: Plan SKU for function-app host. Ignored for other hosts. See azure-functions skill hosting decision table.

durablePattern.description: Durable Functions pattern when host=function-app and the flow requires orchestration. See azure-functions and batch-processing skills.

rationale.description: One-sentence justification for the host choice, sourced from target-architecture.md.

tests.description: Flow-level test cases executed by flow-tester. Each test drives the flow through the in-process interpreter from a fixture payload and compares emitted messages (and optional DLQ envelope) to the declared expectations.

tracked.description: Business/tracked properties emitted on every run of this flow. Packs compile to native primitives (Logic Apps trackedProperties, OTEL span attributes). Replaces the Azure-only trackedProperties template.

metrics.description: Flow-level metrics exported by packs to the platform-native metric sink.

logSampling.description: Log sampling policy. rate is a probability between 0 (sample nothing) and 1 (sample everything). level is the minimum log level to emit after sampling.

trigger.description: Test trigger payload. channel must match the flow's trigger channel. Provide exactly one of path (fixture file) or inline (inline body). headers are optional and populate the same envelope headers a real trigger would.

context.description: Flow-context values surfaced to mapping expressions as $context.<key>. Used to pin normally-generated values (correlation ids, timestamps, uuids) so golden-file assertions are stable.

faults.description: Fault-injection overrides. Force the named step to fail with the given error code; afterAttempts lets the retry logic exercise its budget first.

expect.description: Ordered list of expectations. A 'channel' entry asserts a message was emitted to that channel; a 'step' entry asserts the named step was reached. 'error' asserts that the expected DLQ envelope was produced after N retries.

source.description: Where the property value comes from: a body path (e.g. 'body.orderId'), a header name, or a flow-context reference (e.g. '$context.correlationId').
Body path, header name, or context reference the pack evaluates on each flow run to produce the metric value. For counters the value is typically a literal '1' or a conditional expression.

transform.description: DEPRECATED symbolic transform name. Prefer mappingRef pointing at mappings[].name.

mappingRef.description: Name of a mapping in mappings[] applied by this step. Required on transform and enrich steps going forward.

predicate.description: Filter predicate. Either an engine expression object or a raw string (legacy).

when.description: Route predicate. Either an engine expression object (preferred) or a raw string (legacy).

retry.description: Per-step retry policy. Primarily for invoke steps (today only the flow-level retry applies). When present on other step types, it overrides the flow-level retry for that step.

store.description: claimCheck: name of a channel (typically kind: blob) where the large payload is stored. ir-validator rejects stores whose channel kind is not 'blob'.

keyExpression.description: claimCheck: engine expression (default JSONata) that computes the object-store key from the inbound message body.

referenceMessageRef.description: claimCheck: messages[].name of the reference envelope emitted downstream (typically carrying claim ticket + pointer, not the full payload).

target.description: wireTap: name of the diagnostic channel receiving a duplicate of each message. Non-blocking: failure to publish must not fail the main flow.

rps.description: throttler: requests-per-second cap. Packs compile to the native throttle primitive (API Management, Logic Apps concurrency, etc.).

burst.description: throttler: burst capacity above the sustained rps before strategy kicks in.

strategy.description: throttler behaviour when the limit is exceeded: 'shed' drops (returns 429/quota), 'queue' buffers until capacity frees, 'block' applies backpressure to the caller.

children.description: saga: ordered list of { forward, compensate } pairs. forward is invoked in order; on failure of step N, compensate[N-1..0] run in reverse. Both ids must resolve to steps within the same flow.

orderingKey.description: resequencer: body path or header name used to derive the per-key sequence number. Messages without a key flow through unchanged.

window.description: resequencer: ISO-8601 duration for how long the resequencer buffers out-of-order messages before giving up and forwarding them in arrival order.

codeRef.description: execute: path to the extracted source-code artifact (relative to the IR file). Required on execute steps.

runtime.description: execute / custom mapping: execution environment for the code artifact.

migrationHint.description: execute / custom mapping: pack-facing migration verdict. 'auto' = pack compiles natively; 'local-function' = in-process wrapper; 'azure-function' = sidecar required; 'manual' = STRUCTURAL blocker.

stepErrorHandling.description: Per-step error handling overrides. Mirrors the top-level errorHandling shape and adds step-scoped controls (fallback, retryable/nonRetryable classification, onError disposition).

fallback.description: Id of a step in the same flow to route to on unhandled error. Distinct from the global DLQ. ir-validator rejects fallbacks that don't resolve.

retryableErrors.description: Error classifications (exception type names, HTTP status strings, broker error codes) that should trigger retry. Packs compile to platform-native filters.

nonRetryableErrors.description: Error classifications that must never be retried (e.g. 400 ValidationError). Keeps the retry budget on transient failures.

onError.description: Disposition when the step fails after exhausting retries. 'continue' swallows the error and proceeds to next; 'dlq' sends to the step's (or flow's) dlq; 'fallback' routes to the fallback step; 'fail' propagates to the flow error handler.

tags.description: Free-form labels for cross-referencing (e.g. catalogue IDs like INT-001).

retry.description: Dependency-level retry policy, first-class sibling of timeout. Overrides the flow-level retry when invoke steps target this dependency without their own retry block.

streaming.description: gRPC streaming mode. 'none' is unary; clientStream / serverStream / bidi match the gRPC-native semantics. ir-validator rejects this field on non-grpc dependencies.

idempotencyHeader.description: REST: header the dependency expects for idempotent calls (e.g. 'Idempotency-Key'). Packs propagate the message-level idempotencyKey into this header on every call.

rateLimit.description: REST: dependency-specific rate limit override. Distinct from channel-level rateLimit in channel.binding.

circuitBreaker.description: REST: dependency-specific circuit-breaker override. Distinct from the global errorHandling.circuitBreaker.

driver.description: DB: driver identifier (e.g. 'sqlserver', 'postgres', 'oracle', 'mongodb'). Packs pick the client library from this.

txBoundary.description: DB: transaction boundary. 'perCall' opens a new transaction per invoke step; 'perFlow' opens a single transaction spanning all invoke steps against this dependency within one flow run.

codeRef.description: function: path to the extracted source-code artifact (relative to the IR file). Required when kind is 'function'.

runtime.description: function: execution environment for the callable. Packs compile to the platform-native function host (Logic Apps InvokeFunction, Azure Functions, etc.).

migrationHint.description: function: pack-facing migration verdict. 'auto' = inline; 'local-function' = in-process wrapper; 'azure-function' = sidecar; 'manual' = STRUCTURAL blocker.
